behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the file path (tests/models/smollm3/test_modeling_smollm3.py), the class (SmolLM3IntegrationTest), the method names (test_model_3b_generation, test_model_3b_long_prompt), the old expected text fragment ('pulls objects toward the center of the Earth'), the required parameter (do_sample=False), the forbidden parameter (temperature), and the minimum number of generate() calls (3+). All test assertions map to specifics discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the fix: use do_sample=False as an explicit keyword argument, remove temperature parameter, and remove the specific old expected text. Requirement 2 says 'must include do_sample=False as an explicit keyword argument with literal value False' and Requirement 3 says 'temperature keyword argument must not appear'. This tells the agent exactly WHAT to change rather than just describing what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests check structural properties (do_sample=False present, temperature absent, old text removed, assertions still exist) rather than gold-specific implementation details. An alternative fix that uses do_sample=False, removes temperature, and updates the expected text to any new string would pass. The tests don't check the exact new expected text value.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All fail-to-pass tests (test_dosample_false_in_generate, test_no_temperature_in_generate, test_expected_text_updated) use AST parsing to inspect the source code structure. They don't import, call, or execute the code under test. While the tests note GPU+model requirements as justification, this is still pure text/AST inspection rather than behavioral verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation (e.g., empty method bodies) would fail: test_dosample_false_in_generate requires >=3 generate() calls with do_sample=False, test_expected_text_updated requires assertions in the method body, and test_not_stub requires >=3 meaningful statements per test method. The agent must actually modify the generate() calls properly.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three explicit requirements from instruction.md are tested: (1) old expected text removed (test_expected_text_updated), (2) do_sample=False in all generate calls (test_dosample_false_in_generate), (3) no temperature parameter (test_no_temperature_in_generate).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived), descriptive function names, and detailed docstrings explaining rationale and AST-only justification.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves modifying test code, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, function names, class names, and parameter names are all correctly spelled and match the actual codebase.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact line numbers ('line ~102', 'line ~118'), exact method names, and precise parameter changes needed (do_sample=False, remove temperature).
